mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand growth that drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) or joint positive movements yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during post-shock recovery phases reflect capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate earnings expectations and generate positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during recovery regimes or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that supports corporate revenue projections and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns or Rank IC drops below 0.03 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings that cross above 235.0 trigger inflation concerns that force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 235.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and unemployment rate declines indicates labor market tightening that raises wage pressures and reduces profit margins, driving negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant or predicts positive returns when wage pressures are high.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts that cross below 1.0 million units signal a housing sector downturn that drags on construction spending and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances when housing starts drop below the 1.0 million threshold.
